skill.name: minimalist-ui
skill.description: Guides AI agents to build clean editorial-style interfaces with warm monochrome colors, strong typography, flat grids, and restrained motion.
skill.summary: Create refined minimalist UI designs with editorial typography, flat components, muted accents, and disciplined layout rules.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many severe patterns, but review found they are false positives from markdown inline code and UI design terminology. No executable code, prompt-injection attempt, credential handling, malware behavior, or command execution was found in SKILL.md. The only confirmed concern is a hardcoded third-party placeholder image URL, so the final risk is low.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Spans Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged many inline code examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The referenced lines contain markdown formatting for CSS, Tailwind classes, font names, colors, animation values, and HTML tags. No executable script or command invocation is present in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious backticks are markdown inline-code delimiters around design tokens and CSS examples. There is no shell script, Ruby code, or instruction to execute commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Malware Keyword and Weak Crypto Detections
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged keylogger keywords, weak cryptographic algorithms, and system reconnaissance terms. The relevant lines discuss typography, keyboard UI rendering, CSS shadows, colors, and browser animation APIs. No credential capture, cryptography, host reconnaissance, or malware behavior is described.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged terms are ordinary UI design vocabulary and CSS examples in a markdown skill. I found no semantic evidence of malware, cryptographic use, or reconnaissance intent.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External Placeholder Image URL
low_findings.description: The skill suggests using https://picsum.photos placeholder images when real assets are unavailable. This is not malicious, but generated pages may make third-party network requests if the advice is followed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is explicit and external, but the context is benign placeholder imagery for design mockups. The risk is limited to privacy, availability, and third-party dependency concerns.

content.value_statement: Generic UI generation often produces loud SaaS patterns and inconsistent visual systems. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code precise rules for restrained layouts, typography, color, icons, components, and motion.
actual_capabilities: Defines a warm monochrome palette with muted pastel accent colors.
Specifies editorial typography choices for body text, headings, code, and metadata.
Guides flat bento grids, cards, buttons, tags, accordions, and keyboard UI elements.
Sets iconography and imagery rules for refined technical interfaces.
Provides motion guidance for scroll entry, hover states, and performance-friendly animation.
Gives an execution protocol for applying the style in HTML, React, Tailwind, or Vue work.
limitations: It is a design directive and does not include reusable components or source code files.
It favors one restrained editorial style, so it may not fit playful or highly branded products.
It references premium fonts that may require licensing or fallback choices.
It suggests a third-party placeholder image service when real assets are unavailable.

use_cases.title: Refine a SaaS Dashboard
use_cases.description: Apply a quiet layout system with clear typography, light borders, muted status badges, and restrained interactive states.

use_cases.title: Build an Editorial Landing Page
use_cases.description: Generate a clean page with strong typographic contrast, large whitespace, warm monochrome surfaces, and subtle imagery.

use_cases.title: Standardize AI UI Output
use_cases.description: Give AI agents consistent design constraints before producing React, Tailwind, Vue, or HTML interface work.

prompt_templates.title: Apply the Visual Style
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a small interface
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the minimalist-ui skill to design a clean settings page. Keep the palette warm, the layout flat, and the typography editorial.

prompt_templates.title: Redesign an Existing Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this dashboard and revise it using the minimalist-ui rules. Improve spacing, typography, cards, buttons, status tags, and motion.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Frontend Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a React and Tailwind product page using minimalist-ui. Include bento sections, refined typography, muted accents, accessible states, and subtle scroll reveals.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Design System Pass
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this UI against minimalist-ui, list violations, then update the components to match the palette, type hierarchy, borders, imagery, and animation rules.

output_examples.input: Design a pricing page for a project management product.
output_examples.output: A restrained pricing page with warm off-white sections, crisp bordered plan cards, serif headline treatment, compact muted badges, and subtle hover responses.

output: Simplified navigation with fewer visual weights.
Flat data cards using one-pixel borders and generous padding.
Muted status colors reserved for semantic meaning.
Typography adjusted for scanning and repeated use.

output_examples.input: Create a feature grid for a documentation site.
output_examples.output: An asymmetrical bento grid with white surfaces, charcoal text, crisp dividers, contextual copy, and quiet line-based illustrations.

best_practices: Provide the product type and audience before asking for a design or implementation.
Ask for accessibility, responsive behavior, and realistic content as part of the same request.
Review generated work for font availability, image licensing, and third-party placeholder usage.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill when the brand requires bright gradients, playful illustration, or expressive motion.
Do not treat the skill as a complete component library or design token package.
Do not accept placeholder images in production without replacing or self-hosting them.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps AI agents produce refined minimalist web interfaces with editorial typography, flat layouts, restrained colors, and subtle motion.

faq.question: Does it include production components?
faq.answer: No. It is an instruction skill, not a component library or package of reusable code.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found no executable code or malicious behavior. The only confirmed concern is a low-risk external placeholder URL.

faq.question: Why were static findings reported?
faq.answer: Many findings came from markdown inline code around CSS values, Tailwind classes, colors, and HTML tags.

faq.question: What should users check before production use?
faq.answer: Users should verify font licenses, replace placeholder imagery, and confirm the restrained style fits their brand.
